rulings.Case Title: EST. OF JUSTINE CERRUTI TREADWAY
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #25, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Trustee requests the 1% guideline fee in each subtrust. Trustee has not provided timesheets even after being told in previous accounts to keep timesheets. (LRSF 14.53.) In the prior 24th account order the court reduced the trustee request by the fee paid to the property manager stating these are basic duties of the Trustee and it is included in the guideline fees. a. Petitioner requests trustee fees of $11,904.37 for Guido J. Cerruti subtrust. $2,572.49 appears to have been paid to the property manager. b. Petitioner requests trustee fees of $12,282.57 in Anthony Cerruti subtrust. $2,572.49 appears to have been paid to the property manager. c. Petitioner requests trustee fees of $12,060.57 in Antoinette Calbreath subtrust. $2,575.58 appears to have been paid to the property manager. 2) Attorney is requesting the following fees in each subtrust a. $9,481.25 in Guido Cerruti subtrust. b. $9,481.25 in Anthony Cerruti subtrust. c. $9,606.25 Antoinette Calbreath subtrust 3) Attorney is requesting to pay the following from the reserve: a. Trustee compensation after the date of the account period at the rate of 1% of the FMV of the assets as of the date of the final distribution on a pro-rated monthly basis without further court order. b. Allowing attorney compensation to Watson & Machado LLP from the serve to not exceed $2,500.00. 4) In the Antoinette A. Calbreath subtrust the Examiner requested a receipt for $60.32 for 03/26/2018 Dues/Fees/LIC. No receipt was provided. Examiner recommends reducing trustee fees by $60.32. 5) In the Anthony F. Cerruti subtrust the Examiner requested a receipt for 04/24/2017 Capital Expenditure - Painting for $2,053.51. No receipt was provided. 6) In the Anthony F. Cerruti subtrust the Examiner requested a receipt for 07/25/2017 Capital Expenditure for $172.43. No receipt was provided. 7) In the Guido J. Cerruti subtrust the Examiner requested a receipt for $60.32 for 03/26/2018 Dues/Fees/LIC. No receipt was provided.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MELVIN FLEMING
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/7/2021. 1) This issue still has not been addressed. Now both the 04/01/2021 Supplemental Information and 12/08/2020 Supplemental Information have not verified by Petitioner and must be verified. [CRC 7.103]. 2) Please explain why the caption of the 04/01/2021 Supplemental Information and 04/08/2021 Confidential Statement states Ray Thompson Jr is attorney for self and lists his address when attorney Karen Suri filed a substitution of attorney on 01/29/2021. 3) The proposed order does not have the new attorney's information on the caption and instead has the petitioner's prior pro per information. Please provide an updated proposed order that includes the correct attorney information in the caption. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 5/24/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF MICHAEL DORIA PALACIOS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/7/2021. 1) Please explain and provide legal authority in support of the petition on page 2 line 3.d.(2)(b) saying unknown. The petition must state the annual gross income from personal property. 2) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. 3) Petitioner must file Notice of Petition to Administer Estate (DE-121). 4) Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 5/24/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.]
